---
category: "Upgrade"
---

# Transitioning to the direct Kubernetes runner

This guide covers upgrading Posit Connect helm values from the Launcher (`launcher.enabled: true`) to the direct Kubernetes runner (`backends.kubernetes.enabled: true`).

The direct Kubernetes runner manages content Jobs and Services using standard Kubernetes manifests, replacing the Launcher's template system.

## Minimal upgrade

If you haven't customized `launcher.templateValues`, the minimal upgrade is a single change: set `launcher.enabled: false` and `backends.kubernetes.enabled: true`. Your `sharedStorage` values remain unchanged.

If you customized `config.Launcher`, move those settings to `config.Kubernetes`.
In most cases, you do not need to set `config.Kubernetes.DataDirPVCName` manually: the chart sets it automatically when shared storage is mounted for content (`sharedStorage.create` or `sharedStorage.mount`) and `sharedStorage.mountContent: true`.
Set `DataDirPVCName` explicitly only when you need to override that default behavior (for example, custom PVC naming or `mountContent: false`).

If you customized `launcher.namespace`, move it to `backends.kubernetes.namespace`. If you customized `launcher.defaultInitContainer.*`, the `enabled`, `repository`, `tagPrefix`, and `tag` fields map directly to `backends.kubernetes.defaultInitContainer.*`. The launcher-only fields `imagePullPolicy`, `resources`, and `securityContext` should be set in `defaultResourceJobBase` on the init container spec instead.

```{.yaml include="rstudio-connect-minimal-migration.yaml" filename="values.yaml"}
```

## Upgrading templateValues customizations

If you customized `launcher.templateValues`, those settings move into `backends.kubernetes.defaultResourceJobBase` and `backends.kubernetes.defaultResourceServiceBase`. These are standard Kubernetes [Job](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/job/) and [Service](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/) specs, so Kubernetes documentation applies directly.

The following examples show the same customizations before and after the upgrade. Each field is annotated with a comment pointing to its equivalent in the other file.

**Before** (Launcher-based):

```{.yaml include="rstudio-connect-customized-launcher.yaml" filename="values.yaml (launcher)"}
```

**After** (direct Kubernetes runner):

```{.yaml include="rstudio-connect-customized-migration.yaml" filename="values.yaml (kubernetes)"}
```

## Values with no direct equivalent

| Launcher value | Upgrade path |
| --- | --- |
| `launcher.customRuntimeYaml` | Use `executionEnvironments` instead. See [Execution environments](/charts/rstudio-connect/README.md#execution-environments). |
| `launcher.additionalRuntimeImages` | Use `executionEnvironments` instead. |
| `launcher.launcherKubernetesProfilesConf` | No equivalent. The direct Kubernetes runner does not use profiles. |
| `launcher.templateValues.pod.command` | No direct equivalent override. The direct Kubernetes runner manages `connect-content` command/args at runtime. |
| `launcher.templateValues.pod.securityContext.runAsUser`, `runAsGroup`, `supplementalGroups`; `launcher.templateValues.pod.containerSecurityContext.runAsUser`, `runAsGroup` | No direct equivalent override. The direct Kubernetes runner manages these from Connect's RunAs user/group configuration. |
| `launcher.useTemplates`, `extraTemplates`, `includeDefaultTemplates`, `includeTemplateValues` | N/A. The direct Kubernetes runner uses resource bases instead of templates. |

For complete field-level behavior (`cannot-override`, `merged`, `appended`), see [Kubernetes job overlays](https://docs.posit.co/connect/admin/appendix/off-host/direct-runner/kubernetes-job-overlays.html).

## Important notes

:::{.callout-important}
If you set `serviceAccountName` in the job base, you must label the service account for Connect to use it. Without this label, Connect falls back to the namespace default:

```bash
kubectl label sa <service-account-name> connect.posit.co/service-account=true -n <namespace>
```
:::

**Init container** -- The chart auto-generates a `connect-content-init` init container from `backends.kubernetes.defaultInitContainer`. You do not need to include it in `defaultResourceJobBase` unless you need to override fields not available in `defaultInitContainer` (such as `imagePullPolicy`, `resources`, or `securityContext`). If you do include it, the chart updates its image and ensures the required volume mount. Custom init containers run alongside the auto-generated one.

**Shared storage** -- `sharedStorage.*` values (`create`, `mount`, `mountContent`, `name`, `subPath`, `storageClassName`) work identically for both modes. No changes needed.

**Rollback** -- Set `launcher.enabled: true`, remove `backends.kubernetes`, and rename `config.Kubernetes` back to `config.Launcher`. Previously published content continues to work.
